title: A Brief History of Seven Killings
author: Marlon James

setting.note: Marlon James's many-voiced novel turns on the 1976 attempt on the life of the reggae singer Bob Marley, the Singer, two days before a peace concert, and the political gang warfare of Kingston that lies behind it. The real city is its ground: the Singer's house on Hope Road where the gunmen strike, the ghetto garrisons of West Kingston tied to the rival parties, and the concert in the park; the story later follows the dons and their trade north to New York.

palette.note: The red of the gun violence and the tribal politics that run through Kingston in the book, the blood behind the music. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
